title: Divinity: Original Sin 2 - Definitive Edition

title: Divinity: Original Sin 2 - Definitive Edition

notes:
I had to use the script* everyone else suggested but after that it runs close to perfect. My settings are high (one below ultra) and I get 60 FPS most of the time. There's just one caveat: after triggering a quicksave my FPS tanks down to ~30 and continues dropping with each quicksave. However, alt-tabbing out (the game window disappears since it's fullscreen) and then clicking back into it fixes the problem. This only works if it's done every quicksave. Reducing the number of save files seemed to help a little but not very much, same with disabling cloud sync. 

The script (copy paste and run in steamapps/common/Divinity Original Sin 2): mv ./bin ./bin.bak && ln -s DefEd/bin bin && cd bin && mv ./SupportTool.exe ./SupportTool.bak && ln -s EoCApp.exe SupportTool.exe
title: Divinity: Original Sin 2 - Definitive Edition

notes: Played the game in a single 9 hour sitting. No issues to report runs very well. Only thing is the link method described by others below to get the game started. It is a quick and painless process and afterwards everything works flawlessly.
title: Divinity: Original Sin 2 - Definitive Edition

If it starts, it works flawlessly until it is disrupted. After alt-tabbing out (from window mode), and back again, I have major performance issues. Graphics seems to stabilize, but sound was stuttering. After quitting, I was not able to start the game again. It opens the window, but it is only black. A reboot makes it possible to start the game again. Also was not able to start game via steam if graphics were set to the internal Intel card. Could start with vanilla Wine, but with reduced graphics.

notes: Runs perfectly after symlinking. title: Divinity: Original Sin 2 - Definitive Edition

notes:
Works perfectly after adjustments that were mentioned before, here's the script to do that:

#!/bin/bash 
cd 'path/to/Steam/SteamApps/common/Divinity Original Sin 2'
rm -r bin.bak 
mv bin bin.bak 
ln -s DefEd/bin bin 
rm bin/SupportTool.exe.bak 
mv bin/SupportTool.exe bin/SupportTool.exe.bak 
cp bin/EoCApp.exe bin/SupportTool.exe

title: Divinity: Original Sin 2 - Definitive Edition

notes:
Runs perfectly after this tweak:
* Browse local files
* rename folder bin to bin_bk
* open terminal at the current location
* type `ln -s DefEd/bin bin`
* go inside the new bin shortcut
* Rename SupportTool.exe to SupportTool.exe.bk
* Open terminal at the current location
* type `ln EoCApp.exe SupportTool.exe`
* Launch the game

NOTE: sometimes steam will attempt to update the game, in that case you can go back to how the game was before the tweak, verify the files in case steam got confused and messed up the files. Then re-apply the tweak.

Tested with mods!

notes:
Since the latest update (by 07/14/2019) the following tweak is needed to run the game: go to %game_local_files_path%, remove the bin folder, symlink DefEd folder here as bin, rename the SupportTool.exe to SupportTool.exe.bak and symlink EoCapp.exe as SupportTool.exe there. 
rm -r bin && ln -s DefEd/bin bin && mv bin/SupportTool.exe bin/SupportTool.exe.bak && ln -s bin/EoCapp.exe SupportTool.exe

"winecfg"

title: Divinity: Original Sin 2 - Definitive Edition

notes: The game's launcher that allows you to choose between Classic or Definitive Edition requires a recent version of .NET to work, causing fresh installations of the game to be unplayable. Other posts will mention at least one of two solutions to this. The solution that moves files and creates symbolic links will bypass the launcher, causing Steam to start the Definitive Edition of the game directly. This removes the .NET requirement, but doesn't allow launching the Classic Edition, and it must be reapplied whenever the game updates. The solution that involves installing dotnet472 with winetricks will allow the launcher to work as intended, but this seems to require using winetricks version 20181203 specifically, and will not work with newer or older versions. No matter the solution used, Steam will waste time trying to install .NET again every time the game is launched. In-game, I noticed two issues: The game often fails to generate thumbnails when saving, causing many of my saves to just show blackness in the area where the thumbnails should be, and the Fake Fullscreen mode in the options causes me severe problems with focus when alt-tabbing.

notes: Gold (runs perfectly after tweaks) ### Those tweaks are quick and easy to do. In a terminal, navigate to your Divinity 2 Folder - typically that's something along the lines of ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Divinity\ Original\ Sin\ 2/ - then run the following commands: ### mv ./bin ./bin.bak && ln -s DefEd/bin bin && cd bin && mv ./SupportTool.exe ./SupportTool.bak && ln -s EoCApp.exe SupportTool.exe ### Afterwards it will run just fine. Updates may break things again. You can then either run those commands again, or make a pre-launch script to run them every time you start the game.
title: Divinity: Original Sin 2 - Definitive Edition

notes:
Works great after the "mv ./bin ./bin.bak && ln -s DefEd/bin bin && cd bin && mv ./SupportTool.exe ./SupportTool.bak && ln -s EoCApp.exe SupportTool.exe" tweak. I made no other modifications to the game. 

There can be issues when Alt+Tabbing out of borderless full screen mode into another program where the game is still active and the game's cursor displays on the screen instead of the OS cursor. Alt+Tabbing back to the game and then back to the other program usually fixes this issue though. The other program should be in a different workspace as well if playing in borderless full screen. I've found that Alt+Tabbing to another program in the same workspace that the game is running in results in that program being inaccessible for use while the game is running.

notes: After doing the tweaks mentioned in various different sequences I finally got one that worked for me, there is a slight stutter on my screen, but I am looking into that. The game runs smoothly as far as I've been able to see so far.

title: Divinity: Original Sin 2 - Definitive Edition
Divinity: Original Sin 2 - Definitive Edition

notes:
Finished the game. In 100+ hours played on linux here are the issues I encountered:

-Sometimes launching the game the game stays in the background of the OS UI and has to be killed and started again. Also will sometimes just show a black screen at launch and you have to tab out/in to get the actual image.

-Between act 2&3 and 3&4 the sound cut during the animated cutscene(this might be fixed as the ending cutscene was perfect).

-The game crashed once, after a cutscene where the sound cut off(beginning of act 4).

But tbh in 100+ hours those were so little issues that I consider it platinum worthy.
title: Divinity: Original Sin 2 - Definitive Edition

notes: After playing for about 60 hours with no issues on Manjaro, I recently moved to Arch and had issues running the game. Eventually, I managed to get it working by using the symlink that most other reports suggest, and also installing xact and sdl via protontricks. Without sdl, the game wouldn't make it to the title screen. No issues after a further ~12 hours of gameplay.
title: Divinity: Original Sin 2 - Definitive Edition

notes:
With the tweaks mentioned many times on this page, it runs just fine in multiplayer mode, except:
- You HAVE to run in fullscreen mode. I strongly prefer fullscreened windowed, so that's a knock against "playing perfectly", and
- In three nights of play it had one fatal crash. But that's about in line with what I got on Windows, so again, I think it fits into "minor issues, but playable"
title: Divinity: Original Sin 2 - Definitive Edition

notes: Once the tweak listed in other posts was ran in terminal, everything worked perfectly.
